refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall ensure teleoperation end-to-end communication latency p95 ≤ 150 ms under agreed network conditions to enable safe remote operation.
refined_fr.rationale: The original FR is decomposed to explicitly state the latency metric and its impact on safety, focusing on communication latency as the critical factor for teleoperation safety.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Measure end-to-end latency of teleoperation commands and feedback under defined network conditions and verify p95 latency ≤ 150 ms.
sub_frs: FR1: Minimize communication latency in teleoperation data transmission.
FR2: Ensure reliable and timely telemetry and tasking data exchange.
FR3: Provide latency monitoring and alerting for teleoperation sessions.

candidate_dps: DP1: Use optimized network protocols and compression for telemetry and tasking data.
DP2: Implement edge computing nodes to reduce communication hops and latency.
DP3: Prioritize teleoperation traffic over other data streams in the network.
DP4: Integrate latency monitoring and adaptive control algorithms.
selected_dp.selected_dp: DP1: Use optimized network protocols and compression for telemetry and tasking data.

potential_sources: Telemetry and Tasking data tightly coupled with network link performance
Latency monitoring may couple with Safety Services
coupling_check.notes: DP1 focuses on communication optimization, which is logically separable from other system functions, minimizing coupling.

assumptions_risks: Assumes network conditions are stable and within agreed parameters.
Risk that compression algorithms may introduce processing delay.
Risk that prioritization mechanisms may conflict with other system traffic.
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Consider integrating DP2 (edge computing) if latency targets are not met by protocol optimization alone.

im_boundary.name: Teleoperation
im_boundary.scope: Realizes communication protocol optimization, telemetry and tasking data handling for teleoperation latency control.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Telemetry data exchanged between Teleoperation module and Vehicle module.

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Tasking commands exchanged between Teleoperation module and Vehicle module.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Physical and logical network connection supporting teleoperation data transmission.

module_containment_scores.notes: Broad system scope, low containment specificity for latency optimization.
Vehicle handles telemetry endpoints but not protocol optimization.
Primary module responsible for communication protocol and latency control.
Safety Services monitors alerts but does not implement latency control.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: Latency control logic and communication protocol optimization are best contained within the Teleoperation module to minimize diffusion and maximize ownership clarity.
containment_risks: Potential overlap with Safety Services for latency alerting and safety monitoring.
Dependency on Vehicle module for telemetry endpoints may introduce interface complexity.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Define clear interface contracts with Safety Services for latency alerts and with Vehicle for telemetry data endpoints.

fr_dp_gate.reason: Selected DP logically satisfies the refined FR sub-functions with clear verification paths and manageable coupling.

diffusion_risks: Some diffusion risk exists between Teleoperation and Safety Services for latency alerting.
Interface complexity with Vehicle module for telemetry endpoints.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: Teleoperation module shows high containment score and low diffusion, appropriate for DP realization.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Latency can be measured end-to-end across Teleoperation and Vehicle modules using known interfaces.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Clear ownership of latency control in Teleoperation module with defined external interfaces.

recommended_revisions: Clarify interface contracts between Teleoperation and Safety Services for latency alerting.
Detail telemetry interface specifications with Vehicle module to reduce integration risks.

decision.justification: The current level of decomposition and assignment meets the requirements with clear ownership, manageable coupling, and feasible verification. No further decomposition needed at this stage.

revise.why: Refine interface contracts and ownership boundaries to mitigate diffusion risks.

assumptions: Network conditions remain within agreed parameters.
Telemetry and tasking data volumes are manageable by selected protocols.
open_risks: Potential latency increase due to compression processing overhead.
Interface complexity between Teleoperation and Vehicle modules.
evidence_needed: Latency performance test results under representative network conditions.
Interface specification documents between Teleoperation, Vehicle, and Safety Services.
